steps.title: Identify the appositive
steps.content: In the phrase fellow OECD member nation Chile, the country name identifies which member nation is meant. Because the name is essential to the noun phrase, it should not be set off with punctuation.

steps.title: Reject nonessential punctuation
steps.content: Choice B uses a colon after a phrase that is not an independent clause. Choice C uses a comma, and Choice D uses a dash; both wrongly treat Chile as extra information. The country name should follow nation directly. The answer is A.
